evals.prompt: Show the SSL certificate chain, TLS version, cipher suite, OCSP stapling status, and handshake timing for example.com.
evals.expected_output: Use the certificate-info skill to retrieve raw certificate and TLS connection details without turning the task into a security score.
expectations: Selects certificate-info for raw certificate and TLS connection details.
Does not select certificate-analysis unless the user asks for scoring or a security assessment.
Reports certificate chain, TLS version, cipher suite, OCSP stapling, and handshake timing.

evals.prompt: Check whether a host is vulnerable to Heartbleed, POODLE, ROBOT, FREAK, Logjam, Sweet32, BEAST, CRIME, DROWN, or insecure renegotiation.
evals.expected_output: Use the tls-vulnerability-scanner skill for named TLS exploit probes and report per-vulnerability status.
expectations: Selects tls-vulnerability-scanner for named TLS vulnerability checks.
Does not substitute generic certificate security scanning for TLS exploit probes.
Reports results for the requested vulnerability families.

evals.prompt: Create a CRL signed by my CA that revokes serial numbers 01A3 and 01A4 with key-compromise reason codes.
evals.expected_output: Use the crl-generator skill to generate a signed Certificate Revocation List with the requested serials and reason codes.
expectations: Selects crl-generator for creating a signed Certificate Revocation List.
Uses the requested serial numbers and key-compromise reason code.
Does not route the request to OCSP or generic revocation status checking.

evals.prompt: Use my private CA certificate and key to issue a server authentication certificate for api.internal.example.
evals.expected_output: Use the ca-signer skill to issue a leaf server-authentication certificate from an existing CA certificate and private key.
expectations: Selects ca-signer for issuing a leaf certificate from an existing CA.
Does not select certificate-generator unless the user asks for a self-signed certificate or CA creation.
Preserves the distinction between CA signing and CSR generation.

evals.prompt: Generate authorized phishing detection test certificates for homoglyph and typosquatting variants of example.com.
evals.expected_output: Use the domain-variants skill to generate authorized lookalike-domain certificate test cases and keep the workflow scoped to security research.
expectations: Selects domain-variants for lookalike domain certificate test cases.
Keeps the workflow scoped to authorized security research.
Covers homoglyph and typosquatting variant generation.
